title: Core Framework (rl-agent v2)
description: Lightweight, extensible Go library for building AI-powered applications with zero external dependencies (stdlib only). Core components: Provider (LLM abstraction), Agent (orchestration), Tool (executable functions), Skill (tool bundles), Memory (persistence), HTTP Handler (web exposure).

title: Provider Component
description: LLM abstraction layer with OpenAI implementation. Provides Provider interface for multiple LLM backends.
design: Files: provider/provider.go, provider/openai/openai.go. Provider interface with Complete(), Stream(), Capabilities(). OpenAI implementation using net/http with TLS.

title: Tool Component
description: Executable function registry with fluent builder for tool registration and execution.
design: Files: tool/tool.go, tool/builder.go. Tool interface with Name(), Description(), Parameters(), Execute(). Registry with sync.Map for thread-safety.

title: Agent Component
description: Orchestration engine with tool-calling loop, MaxSteps enforcement, and thread-safe concurrent runs.
design: Files: agent/agent.go, agent/options.go. Agent interface with Run(), Stream(), AddTool(), AddSkill(). Tool-calling loop with MaxSteps enforcement.

title: Skill Component
description: Tool bundles with instructions for grouping related tools and slash command support.
design: Files: skill/skill.go, skill/slash.go. Skill interface with Name(), Description(), Tools(), Instructions(), SlashCommand(). Builder for fluent API.

title: Memory Component
description: Conversation persistence with pluggable backends (InMemory, SQLite, MongoDB).
design: Files: memory/memory.go, memory/inmemory/inmemory.go, memory/sqlite/sqlite.go, memory/mongodb/mongodb.go. Memory interface with Add(), Get(), Search(), Clear().

title: HTTP Handler
description: Web exposure with framework adapters for Gin and Echo.
design: Files: http/handler.go, http/adapters/gin.go, http/adapters/echo.go. Handler interface extending http.Handler. Endpoints: POST /run, POST /stream, GET /tools, GET /health.

title: Compaction
description: Memory size management with truncate, summarize, and archive strategies.
design: Files: memory/compaction.go. Compactor interface with Compact(). Strategies: truncate, summarize, archive. DryRun mode for preview.

title: Additional Providers
description: Anthropic, Gemini, and Zai provider implementations.
design: Files: provider/anthropic/anthropic.go, provider/gemini/gemini.go, provider/zai/zai.go. Provider interface implementations for each LLM API.

title: Examples and Documentation
description: Usage examples (basic, streaming, web-demo) and README documentation.
design: Files: examples/basic/main.go, examples/streaming/main.go, examples/web-demo/main.go, README.md. Demonstrates core usage patterns.

title: Define Provider Types
description: WHY: Need common interface for multiple LLM providers
design: File: provider/provider.go. Define Provider interface (Name, Complete, Stream, Capabilities), CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse, StreamEvent, Message, ToolCall, ProviderCapabilities types.
acceptance_criteria: Provider interface compiles with all methods (FR-001)

title: Stream Patterns and Errors
description: WHY: Need proper stream channel patterns and error handling
design: File: provider/provider.go. Implement stream channel patterns, error wrapping utilities, ProviderError type with retry info.
acceptance_criteria: Errors wrapped with context, streams use proper channel patterns

title: OpenAI Complete Implementation
description: WHY: Need working OpenAI provider for basic completion

items.item: OpenAI client struct with API key
Rate limit handling with retry info

design: File: provider/openai/openai.go. Implement OpenAI client with API key, Complete() using net/http with TLS, request/response JSON, rate limit handling.
acceptance_criteria: Complete() returns valid responses, TLS enforced, no API keys logged

title: OpenAI Stream Implementation
description: WHY: Need streaming support for real-time responses
design: File: provider/openai/openai.go. Implement Stream() with SSE parsing, parse data: lines, yield StreamEvent on channel, close channel on completion/error, handle context cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Stream() yields events and closes channel (FR-002), context respected (FR-010)

title: Provider Contract Tests
description: WHY: Need mock implementations for testing all providers
design: File: provider/provider_test.go. Create mock provider implementation, test interface compliance, test all Provider methods.

title: OpenAI Tests with Fixtures
description: WHY: Need reproducible tests without live API calls
design: File: provider/openai/openai_test.go. Record HTTP fixtures, test Complete() with various responses, test Stream() event handling, test error cases (rate limit, timeout, invalid response).
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass with recorded fixtures (SC-007)

title: Rate Limit Edge Case
description: WHY: Need graceful handling when provider API is rate limited
design: File: provider/openai/openai.go. Implement rate limit detection, return wrapped error with retry-after info.
acceptance_criteria: Rate limit returns wrapped error with retry info

title: Define Tool Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for executable functions that agents can call
design: File: tool/tool.go. Define Tool interface (Name, Description, Parameters, Execute), ToolInfo struct, ToolParameter struct with JSON schema fields.
acceptance_criteria: Tool interface defines all required methods (FR-003)

title: Fluent Tool Builder
description: WHY: Need intuitive API for creating tools
design: File: tool/builder.go. Implement ToolBuilder struct, New(name) constructor, Description(desc), Param(name, typ, desc, required), Action(fn), Build() with validation.

title: Tool Registry Implementation
description: WHY: Need thread-safe registry to manage tools
design: File: tool/tool.go. Define ToolRegistry interface, implement with sync.Map for thread-safety, implement Register, Get, List, ToProviderTools methods.
acceptance_criteria: Registry thread-safe, all operations working (FR-003, FR-008)

title: Tool Definition Conversion
description: WHY: Need to convert tools to provider-compatible format

items.item: Conversion from Tool to ToolDefinition working

design: File: tool/tool.go. Implement ToolDefinition struct for provider tools, implement conversion from Tool to ToolDefinition.

title: Schema Validation
description: WHY: Need to validate tool parameters before execution

items.item: Validation error returned for invalid params

design: File: tool/tool.go. Implement JSON schema validation for tool parameters, validate required fields present, validate types match schema.
acceptance_criteria: Schema validation rejects invalid parameters before execution (FR-009)

title: Tool Tests
description: WHY: Need comprehensive tests for tool functionality
design: File: tool/tool_test.go. Test registry Register/Get/List operations, test builder with valid/invalid configs, test validation logic, test concurrent access with -race.

title: Define Agent Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for orchestrating provider, tools, and memory
design: File: agent/agent.go. Define Agent interface (Run, Stream, AddTool, AddSkill), RunResult struct with Content, ToolCalls, FinishReason, StreamResult struct.
acceptance_criteria: Agent interface defines all required methods (FR-002, FR-003)

title: Agent Config and Constructor
description: WHY: Need configuration with sensible defaults
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement AgentConfig struct with defaults (MaxSteps=10, Temperature=0.7), NewAgent() constructor with provider.

title: Functional Options
description: WHY: Need flexible configuration pattern for agent runs
design: File: agent/options.go. Implement RunOption type, RunConfig struct, options: WithSystemPrompt, WithTools, WithSkills, WithMaxSteps, WithSession, WithMemory.
acceptance_criteria: Functional options pattern working (FR-003, FR-004, FR-005)

title: Stream Implementation
description: WHY: Need streaming support for real-time UI updates
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement Stream(), forward provider stream events, generate tool execution events, handle tool results in stream context.
acceptance_criteria: Stream() forwards provider events + tool events (FR-002)

title: MaxSteps Enforcement
description: WHY: Need to prevent infinite loops
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement MaxSteps limit check in loop, return result with FinishReason=max_steps when exceeded.

title: Context Cancellation
description: WHY: Need to support graceful shutdown at all levels
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement context cancellation at all levels, pass context to provider calls, pass context to tool execution, ensure immediate stop on context cancel.

title: Thread Safety
description: WHY: Need to support concurrent agent runs
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement thread-safe concurrent runs with sync primitives, protect shared state with mutexes.

title: User Story 1 Test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for basic completion
design: File: agent/agent_test.go. Implement TestUserStory1_BasicCompletion: Scenario 1: agent.Run returns RunResult with content, Scenario 2: response respects system prompt.

title: Run Implementation
description: WHY: Need to execute the agent with tool-calling loop
design: File: agent/agent.go. Implement Run() with tool-calling loop: build request with system prompt + messages + tools, call provider.Complete, handle tool calls: execute tools, add results to messages, loop until no tool calls or MaxSteps reached.
acceptance_criteria: Run() executes complete tool-calling loops (FR-002, FR-003)

title: User Story 2 Test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for tool calling

items.item: Scenario 1: Tool called with correct args

design: File: agent/agent_test.go. Implement TestUserStory2_ToolCalling: Scenario 1: tool called with correct arguments, Scenario 2: tool error handled gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Test passes with mock provider and tools

title: User Story 3 Test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for streaming
design: File: agent/agent_test.go. Implement TestUserStory3_Streaming: Scenario 1: receive channel of StreamEvents, Scenario 2: context cancellation closes stream cleanly.

title: Success Criteria Tests
description: WHY: Need to verify latency and enforcement criteria
design: File: agent/agent_test.go. Implement TestSC001_CompletionLatency (basic completion < 5s), TestSC002_MaxStepsEnforcement (tool loop completes within MaxSteps).

title: Define Skill Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for bundling tools with instructions
design: File: skill/skill.go. Define Skill interface (Name, Description, Tools, Instructions, SlashCommand), SkillInfo struct.
acceptance_criteria: Skill interface defines all required methods (FR-007)

title: Skill Builder
description: WHY: Need fluent API for creating skills
design: File: skill/skill.go. Implement SkillBuilder, New(name) constructor, Description, WithTool, WithInstruction, AsSlashCommand, Build methods with validation.

title: Slash Command Types
description: WHY: Need types for Discord/Slack integration
design: File: skill/slash.go. Define SlashCommandDefinition struct, SlashOption struct, OptionType constants (String, Integer, Boolean, etc.), WithSlashOption() builder method.

title: InMemory Backend
description: WHY: Need default in-memory storage for testing
design: File: memory/inmemory/inmemory.go. Implement thread-safe storage with sync.RWMutex, Add/Get/Search/Clear methods with chronological ordering for Get, Search returns empty (unsupported).

title: SQLite Backend
description: WHY: Need local persistence option
design: File: memory/sqlite/sqlite.go. Implement schema creation (entries table with indexes), all Memory methods with SQL queries, Search with LIKE query, handle connection pooling.

title: InMemory Tests
description: WHY: Need to verify InMemory-specific functionality
design: File: memory/inmemory/inmemory_test.go. Test concurrent access with -race, thread-safety guarantees, TestSC003_MemoryGetLatency (100 entries < 10ms).

title: User Story 4 Test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for memory integration
design: File: memory/memory_test.go. Implement TestUserStory4_Memory: Scenario 1: messages persisted after run, Scenario 2: previous context included in subsequent runs.

title: Skill Tests
description: WHY: Need to verify skill functionality
design: File: skill/skill_test.go. Test builder with multiple tools, test instruction integration, test slash command metadata.

title: Define Memory Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for conversation persistence
design: File: memory/memory.go. Define Memory interface (Add, Get, Search, Clear), Entry struct (ID, Role, Content, Metadata, Timestamp), MemoryBackend interface (Connect, Close, Memory), all methods accept context for cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Memory interface defines all required methods (FR-005, FR-010)

title: Memory Interface Tests
description: WHY: Need backend-agnostic tests
design: File: memory/memory_test.go. Create backend-agnostic test suite, test Add/Get/Clear operations, chronological ordering, context cancellation.
acceptance_criteria: Backend-agnostic tests pass for all backends (SC-005)

title: Handler Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for HTTP exposure
design: File: http/handler.go. Define Handler interface extending http.Handler, RunRequest, RunResponse, StreamRequest, ErrorResponse structs.
acceptance_criteria: Handler interface defines all required types (FR-006)

title: Endpoints Implementation
description: WHY: Need HTTP endpoints for agent operations
design: File: http/handler.go. Implement POST /run (returns JSON RunResult), POST /stream (SSE text/event-stream), GET /tools (lists tools), POST /tools (executes tool), GET /health (200 OK).

title: CORS and JSON Support
description: WHY: Need CORS and JSON handling

items.item: Error response formatting with status codes

design: File: http/handler.go. Implement CORS middleware with headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Methods, Headers), JSON request parsing, JSON response writing, error response formatting with proper status codes.
acceptance_criteria: CORS headers present, JSON handling correct (FR-006)

title: Echo Adapter
description: WHY: Need Echo framework integration
design: File: http/adapters/echo.go. Implement EchoHandler(agent Agent) echo.HandlerFunc function, delegate to standard handler.

title: Handler Tests
description: WHY: Need to verify HTTP compliance
design: File: http/handler_test.go. Use httptest for endpoint testing, test all endpoints, test SSE streaming, test CORS headers, implement TestSC006_HTTPCompliance.

title: User Story 5 Test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for HTTP API
design: File: http/handler_test.go. Implement TestUserStory5_HTTP: Scenario 1: POST /run returns JSON RunResult, Scenario 2: POST /stream returns SSE events.

title: Compactor Interface
description: WHY: Need interface for memory compaction
design: File: memory/compaction.go. Define Compactor interface with Compact(ctx, opts) (CompactStats, error), CompactOptions struct (Strategy, DryRun, MaxEntries, MaxAge), CompactStats struct (EntriesBefore, EntriesAfter, BytesSaved, Duration), CompactStrategy constants (Truncate, Summarize, Archive).

title: Compaction Strategies
description: WHY: Need implementations for truncate, summarize, and archive

items.item: DryRun mode returns stats without modifying

design: File: memory/compaction.go. Implement truncate strategy (remove oldest entries beyond threshold), summarize strategy (requires provider for summarization), archive strategy (move old entries to archive storage), DryRun mode (return stats without modifying).

title: Gemini Provider
description: WHY: Need Google AI support
design: File: provider/gemini/gemini.go. Implement Google AI format for messages (contents array), Complete() with Gemini API (generateContent endpoint), Stream() with Gemini streaming format, handle Gemini-specific tool/function calling format.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements interface, no API keys logged, TLS enforced (FR-001, FR-011, FR-012)

title: Anthropic Provider
description: WHY: Need Claude API support
design: File: provider/anthropic/anthropic.go. Implement Claude API format for messages (system, user, assistant), Complete() with Anthropic API (messages endpoint), Stream() with Anthropic SSE format, handle Claude-specific tool calling format.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements interface, no API keys logged, TLS enforced (FR-001, FR-011, FR-012)

title: Additional Provider Tests
description: WHY: Need reproducible tests for all providers
design: Files: provider/anthropic/anthropic_test.go, provider/gemini/gemini_test.go, provider/zai/zai_test.go. Record HTTP fixtures, test Complete() with various responses, test Stream() event handling, test error cases (rate limit, timeout, invalid response).
acceptance_criteria: All providers tested with recorded fixtures (SC-007)

title: Handler Tests
description: WHY: Need httptest-based endpoint testing
design: File: http/handler_test.go. Use httptest for endpoint testing, test all endpoints, test SSE streaming, test CORS headers, implement TestSC006_HTTPCompliance.

title: Compaction Tests
description: WHY: Need to verify memory compaction functionality

items.item: Compact() safe on run on compaction on live data

design: File: memory/compaction_test.go. Implement Test for memory/compaction.go, Test all three strategies

title: User story 4 acceptance test
description: WHY: Need acceptance test for memory integration

items.item: Compact() safe on run on compaction on live data
archive strategy moves old entries to separate storage

design: File: memory/memory_test.go. Implement Test for memory/compaction.go. test all three strategies

title: SQLite backend
description: WHY: Need local persistence option
design: File: memory/sqlite/sqlite.go. Implement schema creation, entries table with indexes, handle connection pooling, SQLite backend using database/sql.
acceptance_criteria: SQLite backend functional with In-memory backend

title: user story 5 acceptance test
description: WHY: need acceptance test for memory integration

items.item: test for memory/compaction_test.go. verify compact() safe on runim compaction

design: File: memory/memory_test.go. implement Test for memory/compaction.go, test all three strategies

title: User story 5 test
description: WHY: need acceptance test for HTTP API
design: File: http/handler_test.go, http/adapters/gin.go, echo.go. Use httptest for endpoint.

title: Examples tests
description: WHY: need tests for examples to run
design: Files: examples/basic/main.go, examples/streaming/main.go, examples/web-demo/main.go

title: streaming example
description: WHY: need streaming example for real-time UI updates
design: file: examples/streaming/main.go. http.HandlerFunc for streaming with event handling

description: WHY: need usage examples and quickstart documentation
design: Update README.md with installation and quickstart sections

title: Compaction strategies
description: WHY: Need memory size management strategies
design: file: memory/compaction.go. Implement three strategies ( dry-run mode

title: edge case tests
description: WHY: Need edge case tests
design: Files: memory/compaction_test.go, agent/agent_test.go. Implement edge case tests

design: Files: examples/basic/main.go, examples/streaming/main.go, README.md

design: files: examples/basic/main.go, examples/streaming/main.go, README.md

design: file: memory/compaction_test.go, implement test for memory/compaction strategies

design: files: http/handler_test.go, http/adapters

description: WHY: need gemini provider implementation
design: file: provider/gemini/gemini.go. implement Gemini API format

description: WHY: need tests for anthropic, gemini, zai providers
design: files: provider/gemini/gemini_test.go

title: Examples documentation
description: WHY: need usage examples and documentation

items.item: Web demo example compiles and runs
README covers installation, quickstart, API overview

design: Files: examples/basic/main.go, examples/streaming/main.go, README.md. Simple agent with one tool, show streaming example, demonstrate web integration.
acceptance_criteria: All examples compile and run, README covers core usage patterns
